confirmed.title: Negative backoff values crash retry loop with unhandled ValueError
confirmed.verification: Verified at src/reasoning_client.py:108,111,569,572,579. Configuration loading (lines 108, 111) does not clamp BACKOFF_BASE or RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF to non-negative, unlike MAX_RETRIES (line 107). Negative values produce negative sleep arguments: line 569 calculates RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(rate_attempts+1), and line 579 calculates BACKOFF_BASE**transient_attempts. Tested: time.sleep(-30.0) and time.sleep(-1.5) both raise ValueError("sleep length must be non-negative"). The exception handlers (lines 561-580) catch only PermanentReasoningError, RateLimitError, and ReasoningError—not ValueError. Crash is unhandled and complete() function fails. Not in WONTFIX list (HARDENING_LOG.md lines 287-301). Not tested in test_reasoning_client.py.
confirmed.recommended_action: Apply the proposed fix: clamp both BACKOFF_BASE and RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF at configuration time using max(0.0, float(...)) on lines 108 and 111, matching the pattern used for MAX_RETRIES on line 107. This ensures calculations on lines 569 and 579 always produce non-negative sleep values, preventing ValueError crashes.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Clamp BACKOFF_BASE and RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF to non-negative values at configuration time, similar to how MAX_RETRIES is clamped on line 107: `BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env(...)))` and `RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env(...)))`. This ensures the calculations on lines 569 and 579 always produce non-negative sleep values, preventing ValueError crashes.

confirmed.title: pyproject.toml dev dependencies partially lack upper bounds (pytest/pytest-cov only)
confirmed.verification: Verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/pyproject.toml:26-32: dev extras specify pytest>=8.2.2 and pytest-cov>=5.0.0 with NO upper bounds. Verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/requirements-ci.txt:31-32: same tools have upper bounds (pytest>=8.2.2,<9, pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6). uv.lock confirms divergence: pytest resolves to 9.0.3, pytest-cov to 7.1.0. Pass-5 batch 16 (commit 46a67d3) added upper bounds to requirements-ci.txt with comment "#264 pattern, pass-5" but pyproject.toml was never updated. HOWEVER, the candidate's claim is INCOMPLETE/MISLEADING: it falsely claims that ruff and black are bounded in requirements-ci.txt (they are not listed there at all). CI workflow (.github/workflows/test.yml:63) installs ruff==0.4.8 separately as an exact pin for linting only, not from requirements-ci.txt. ruff and black are dev/tooling dependencies, not test suite dependencies, so the analogy to pytest/pytest-cov is broken.
confirmed.recommended_action: Add upper bounds to pyproject.toml for ONLY pytest and pytest-cov to match requirements-ci.txt (pytest>=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6). Do NOT add ruff or black bounds to pyproject.toml — these are tooling dependencies intentionally excluded from requirements-ci.txt. The inconsistency for pytest/pytest-cov is real but LOW severity: it only affects developers using `pip install hc-grc[dev]` without requirements-ci.txt, and the fix is straightforward. The candidate's proposed fix over-corrects by including ruff/black, suggesting misunderstanding of the tooling/test dependency separation.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Add upper bounds to pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies].dev to match requirements-ci.txt: change pytest>=8.2.2 to pytest>=8.2.2,<9; pytest-cov>=5.0.0 to pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6; ruff>=0.4.8 to ruff>=0.4.8,<1; black>=24.4.2 to black>=24.4.2,<25. This ensures that developers installing via any path (requirements.txt, requirements-ci.txt, or pyproject.toml[dev]) get version-compatible tool sets and CI tests the same major versions as production.

confirmed.title: is_available(Tier.T2) makes unvalidated HTTP request to T2_BASE_URL, violating ADR-0002 loopback enforcement
confirmed.verification: Read /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/reasoning_client.py lines 740-779 (is_available function) and lines 249-291 (_t2_complete function). Confirmed: is_available(Tier.T2) calls urllib.request.urlopen(f"{T2_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=3) at line 759 with NO prior loopback validation. By contrast, _t2_complete() correctly calls require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, ...) at line 254 before any network operations. The require_local_uri() function (src/infrastructure/config.py:47-60) validates HTTP/HTTPS URIs have loopback hostnames per ADR-0002. This is reachable: if HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL env var is set to a remote host, is_available(Tier.T2) will make an HTTP request to that remote host. The function is public (listed in docstring line 40) and called in tests. The code comment at line 251-252 of _t2_complete() explicitly states the rationale: "ADR-0002: the prompt is SCF-derived — refuse to send it to a non-loopback Ollama endpoint (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL could point off-box) (pass-7)." This same reasoning applies to is_available(): an environment variable misconfiguration should be caught defensively at the point of use. The Pass 7 HARDENING_LOG (lines 276-279) mentions that loopback validation was added to T2_BASE_URL, but inspection shows it only applies to _t2_complete(), not is_available().
confirmed.recommended_action: Add loopback validation to is_available(Tier.T2) before the urllib.request.urlopen() call at line 759. Insert the validation immediately after the "if tier is Tier.T2:" block entry (line 753), matching the pattern in _t2_complete(): import require_local_uri and call require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL) availability check") before any network operations. This closes a defense-in-depth gap and ensures consistent ADR-0002 enforcement across all T2 entry points.
confirmed.proposed_fix:
Add loopback validation to is_available(Tier.T2) before the urllib.request.urlopen() call. Insert this line before line 759:
    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
    require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL) availability check")
This mirrors the validation in _t2_complete() and ensures the function refuses to probe non-loopback hosts, maintaining the ADR-0002 boundary.
